title: Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications
subtitle: The estimated death rate from [neonatal](#dod:neonatal) [preterm birth complications](#dod:preterm-birth-complications) in children under five years old, per 100,000.
caption:
Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications

The estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old, per 100,000.

The 2023 choropleth map displays global variation in mortality rates using a color scale ranging from pale yellow (0) to dark blue (1,000 per 100,000). The highest values, represented by the darkest blue shades corresponding to the 200 to 1,000 bands, are concentrated across Sub-Saharan Africa. Countries such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Somalia fall within these upper ranges, with several nations in West and Central Africa appearing in the deepest blue category above 500. Moving northward, North African nations and those in the Middle East generally occupy the middle bands, with colors indicating rates roughly between 20 and 100. In Asia, India and Pakistan show values in the 50 to 100 range, while China and Russia appear in lighter teal shades, suggesting rates between 10 and 20. Europe is predominantly colored in the lightest bands, with most countries falling between 0 and 10; nations like Norway and Iceland are in the palest yellow category near zero. Similarly, Australia, Canada, and the United States are shaded in light teal or pale yellow, placing them in the 0 to 20 range. South America shows a mix, with Brazil and Argentina in the lower bands around 10 to 20, while some northern countries sit slightly higher. Greenland is marked with a hatched pattern, indicating no data is available for that region. The visual gradient transitions from the high-intensity blues in central Africa to the pale yellows and light greens across the Northern Hemisphere and Oceania.
